body.title: Human review of Gemini + Opus audit prompts before kickoff — no silent model-of-the-audit
body.result: Akien reviewed + edited pass1_gemini_prompt.md + pass2_opus_subagent_prompt.md. Edits shipped in 811718cf. Review gate satisfied. Pass 3 disposition still pending — Akien to decide whether it needs a separate prompt file or is manual synthesis.
body.description:
Akien 2026-04-19: 'I wanna read the prompt for each of them before we press go.' Policy: the prompts that shape Pass 1 (Gemini) and Pass 2 (Opus) of the three-pass audit are themselves load-bearing — they decide what gets found and how it's framed. Akien reads and approves each prompt before the corresponding pass runs. No pass kicks without explicit green light.

## Scope
- CC drafts Pass-1 prompt (Gemini breadth, multi-persona auditor, biomimicry lens, no skip-rules)
- CC drafts Pass-2 prompt template (used for each parallel subagent — there will be 4-8, one per concern category)
- Both prompts go into lab/design_docs/audit_2026/ as markdown files so they're versioned in git
- Akien reads, edits, approves
- Only then does kickoff happen
- Pass-3 prompt is trivially different (me synthesizing, no external call); Akien still reviews the synthesis framing

## Why
Prompts are the shape of the lens. A prompt that says 'skip truisms' (see T-extract-prompt-zero-tolerance) produces systematically zero output. A prompt that under-specifies the biomimicry lens produces generic code-review output. We need Akien's eyes on the prompts because the prompts are the audit's model of what-matters.

## Acceptance
- lab/design_docs/audit_2026/pass1_gemini_prompt.md exists with CC draft, Akien edits
- lab/design_docs/audit_2026/pass2_opus_subagent_prompt.md exists, approved
- Audit kickoff commands reference the approved prompts by path
- No kickoff before explicit approval ('press go')

## Related
- T-three-pass-audit (parent; timing = end-of-sprint per 2026-04-19 interview)

## Provenance
Akien 2026-04-19 interview.
